iterations.description: Raw first draft, no AI assistance
Typo fixes and inline links added
changes: Fixed typos and spelling errors
Added hyperlinks (Sefaria, Suno, Typst)

iterations.description: Expanded examples and added reliability narrative
changes: Added audio/video editing examples (MLT/melt, normalising, destemming)
Expanded Claude Spaces concept with link and explanation
Added new section on Claude 4.6 reliability as the biggest transformation
Added closing links to Projects Index and claude.danielrosehill.com
Various additions and tweaks added by hand
Added multi-agent orchestration (Agent UN) and synthetic personas example
Added post-digestive-surgery diet planning example
Added medical note summarisation for doctor's appointments
Added monthly budgeting as anxiety-reduction example
Added small claims evidence gathering against abusive landlord, including the irony of landlord claiming photos were AI-generated

iterations.description: Restructured for logical flow and added subheadings
changes: Reorganised paragraphs into logical thematic sections
Added subheadings: Tearing Down the Moat, Beyond Code, The Terminal Paradox, Reliability Over Brilliance, Making Agentic AI Approachable, Words Are My Code, Worth Every Penny
Grouped all personal use-case examples together under Beyond Code
Consolidated CLI/UI discussion under The Terminal Paradox
Moved value/cost discussion into its own closing section
Removed orphaned/incomplete paragraph about travel agent system prompt
Minor typo fixes (converegence, knoweldge, indispensible, efficienc, built→build)

iterations.description: Added personalised software thesis and learning motivation theme
changes: Added paragraph on personalised software era after the software company/cost discussion - covering the shift from centralised mass-market software to decentralised end-user generation and its democratising effect

Added paragraph after Making Agentic AI Approachable section on Claude Code as a motivator to learn more about technology, countering the 'skills degradation' worry, and introducing the 'do and then teach' use-case pattern
